behavior_summary: TROODLE::look runs when the Troodle is 'looked at' by the player/actor. It marks/configures the target process (the Troodle instance) with an info key and a process-exclude flag, then causes the Troodle to 'speak' a short description beginning with the literal "changeling ".

To produce the speech it allocates/constructs the string "changeling ", passes the string pointer and the target process PID to Item::bark, frees the temporary string, captures the bark call's return value, applies an 'implies' operation to that return value and stores the result in a temporary local, and finally suspends the script.

Overall effect in gameplay: when examined, the Troodle will announce itself with the word 'changeling' (or similar message) and the script prepares/excludes the object from certain processing, likely to avoid repeated handling or to set an internal state.
notable_control_flow: Entry: Event 0 ('U8P_TROODLE::look') triggers TROODLE::look and supplies the target process/item PID as a parameter.
The routine performs two engine-state operations in sequence (push info id 0x0208 with a referenced object, then a 'process exclude') before invoking Item::bark.

After calling Item::bark, the routine frees the allocated string, inspects the bark return via an 'implies' opcode, pops that result into a temp local, and then suspends — so the script does not continue running immediately after the U8P_TROODLE::look handler completes.
No branching or loops are present; the flow is linear and ends with suspension.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the 'set info' id 0x0208 used here: what game state or flag does 0x0208 represent for this object?
Precise effect and intended scope of 'process exclude' in this context — does it prevent further events for this object or only certain engine processing?
Meaning and purpose of the 'implies' opcode applied to Item::bark's return value: is it a boolean test, a propagation of success, or a control hint?
The indirect two-byte value pushed before 'set info' — which specific referenced object does it denote (the Troodle itself or another related instance)?

main_function.name: U8P_TROODLE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the examine/U8P_TROODLE::look event for a Troodle instance: set object info/exclusion state and make the item emit a short descriptive message ('changeling ').
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_TROODLE::look) — triggered when the player/actor looks at or inspects the Troodle (the engine invokes this handler with the target process PID).

function_map.name: U8P_TROODLE::look
function_map.role: Event handler for 'U8P_TROODLE::look' examine behavior; sets object info/exclusion then triggers Item::bark to present a short description.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/external routine invoked to make a process/item emit/display a short text message; called with (target_pid, char* message).

internal_calls.purpose: Configure object info (id 0x0208) on a referenced object and mark the process with an exclusion flag to alter further processing; prevents or changes subsequent handling of the object (inferred).

external_calls.purpose: Request the target process/item to emit/display a short description string starting with 'changeling '. This is the gameplay-facing speech/description shown when the object is examined.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: TROODLE::look runs when the Troodle is 'looked at' by the player/actor. It marks/configures the target process (the Troodle instance) with an info key and a process-exclude flag, then causes the Troodle to 'speak' a short description beginning with the literal "changeling ".
